framework.name: SOC 2 (AICPA Trust Services Criteria)
framework.description: The AICPA Trust Services Criteria used in SOC 2 examinations, covering security (Common Criteria) plus the opt-in categories of availability, confidentiality, processing integrity, and privacy. Each criterion is a mappable control objective an organization is evaluated against.

metadata.scope: Security/Common Criteria (CC1-CC9) are always in scope; Availability, Confidentiality, Processing Integrity, and Privacy are opt-in categories selected per assessment.

requirements.name: CC1.1 — Commitment to integrity and ethical values
requirements.description: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values throughout the organization.

requirements.name: CC1.2 — Board independence and oversight
requirements.description: The board of directors operates independently and exercises oversight of the development and performance of internal control.

requirements.name: CC1.3 — Management structures, reporting lines, and authority
requirements.description: Management establishes structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities in pursuit of objectives.

requirements.name: CC1.4 — Commitment to competence
requirements.description: The entity commits to attracting, developing, and retaining competent individuals aligned with its objectives.

requirements.name: CC1.5 — Accountability for internal control responsibilities
requirements.description: The entity holds individuals accountable for their internal control responsibilities in pursuit of objectives.

requirements.name: CC2.1 — Quality information to support internal control
requirements.description: The entity obtains or generates relevant, quality information to support the functioning of internal control.

requirements.name: CC2.2 — Internal communication of objectives and responsibilities
requirements.description: The entity internally communicates information, including control objectives and responsibilities, needed to support internal control.

requirements.name: CC2.3 — External communication of relevant matters
requirements.description: The entity communicates with external parties about matters affecting the functioning of internal control.

requirements.name: CC3.1 — Objectives specified to enable risk identification
requirements.description: The entity specifies objectives with sufficient clarity to enable the identification and assessment of related risks.

requirements.name: CC3.2 — Identification and analysis of risk
requirements.description: The entity identifies and analyzes risks to the achievement of its objectives as a basis for determining how risks should be managed.

requirements.name: CC3.3 — Consideration of fraud risk
requirements.description: The entity considers the potential for fraud when assessing risks to the achievement of objectives.

requirements.name: CC3.4 — Identification and assessment of change
requirements.description: The entity identifies and assesses changes that could significantly affect its system of internal control.

requirements.name: CC4.1 — Ongoing and separate evaluations
requirements.description: The entity selects, develops, and performs ongoing and separate evaluations to ascertain whether controls are present and functioning.

requirements.name: CC4.2 — Evaluation and communication of deficiencies
requirements.description: The entity evaluates and communicates internal control deficiencies in a timely manner to those responsible for corrective action.

requirements.name: CC5.1 — Control activities that mitigate risk
requirements.description: The entity selects and develops control activities that contribute to mitigating risks to acceptable levels.

requirements.name: CC5.2 — Control activities over technology
requirements.description: The entity selects and develops general control activities over technology to support the achievement of objectives.

requirements.name: CC5.3 — Deployment through policies and procedures
requirements.description: The entity deploys control activities through policies that establish expectations and procedures that put those policies into action.

requirements.name: CC6.1 — Logical access security software and infrastructure
requirements.description: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures to protect information assets from unauthorized access.

requirements.name: CC6.2 — User registration and authorization
requirements.description: The entity registers and authorizes new users and removes access when no longer required, governing credentials throughout their lifecycle.

requirements.name: CC6.3 — Role-based access and least privilege
requirements.description: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access based on roles and least privilege to protect information assets.

requirements.name: CC6.4 — Physical access restriction
requirements.description: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected information assets to authorized personnel.

requirements.name: CC6.5 — Logical and physical protections on asset disposal
requirements.description: The entity discontinues logical and physical protections over assets only after rendering the data they contain unrecoverable.

requirements.name: CC6.6 — Protection against external threats
requirements.description: The entity implements controls to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.

requirements.name: CC6.7 — Restriction of information transmission and movement
requirements.description: The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorized users and protects it during transit.

requirements.name: CC6.8 — Prevention/detection of unauthorized or malicious software
requirements.description: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software.

requirements.name: CC7.1 — Detection of configuration changes and vulnerabilities
requirements.description: The entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify configuration changes and new vulnerabilities.

requirements.name: CC7.2 — Monitoring for anomalies and security events
requirements.description: The entity monitors system components for anomalies indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors, and analyzes security events.

requirements.name: CC7.3 — Evaluation of security events and incident determination
requirements.description: The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they constitute incidents and warrant a response.

requirements.name: CC7.4 — Incident response program
requirements.description: The entity responds to identified security incidents through a defined program that contains, remediates, and recovers from them.

requirements.name: CC7.5 — Recovery from identified incidents
requirements.description: The entity identifies, develops, and implements activities to recover from identified security incidents.

requirements.name: CC8.1 — Change management over infrastructure, data, and software
requirements.description: The entity authorizes, designs, develops, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures.

requirements.name: CC9.1 — Risk mitigation activities for business disruptions
requirements.description: The entity identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities for risks arising from potential business disruptions.

requirements.name: CC9.2 — Vendor and business partner risk management
requirements.description: The entity assesses and manages risks associated with vendors and business partners.

requirements.name: A1.1 — Capacity management
requirements.description: The entity maintains, monitors, and evaluates current processing capacity and demand to support availability objectives.

requirements.name: A1.2 — Environmental protections, backup, and recovery infrastructure
requirements.description: The entity authorizes, designs, and maintains environmental protections, backups, and recovery infrastructure to meet availability objectives.

requirements.name: A1.3 — Recovery plan testing
requirements.description: The entity tests recovery plan procedures to confirm they support the achievement of availability objectives.

requirements.name: C1.1 — Identification and maintenance of confidential information
requirements.description: The entity identifies and maintains confidential information to meet its confidentiality objectives.

requirements.name: C1.2 — Disposal of confidential information
requirements.description: The entity disposes of confidential information when no longer needed to meet its confidentiality objectives.

requirements.name: PI1.1 — Quality information about processing objectives
requirements.description: The entity obtains or generates relevant, quality information regarding the objectives of its processing.

requirements.name: PI1.2 — Completeness and accuracy of inputs
requirements.description: The entity implements controls over system inputs to ensure they are complete and accurate.

requirements.name: PI1.3 — Completeness and accuracy of processing
requirements.description: The entity implements controls to ensure that processing of data is complete, accurate, and timely.

requirements.name: PI1.4 — Completeness and accuracy of outputs
requirements.description: The entity implements controls to ensure that outputs are complete, accurate, and delivered to the intended recipients.

requirements.name: PI1.5 — Integrity of stored inputs and outputs
requirements.description: The entity stores inputs and outputs completely, accurately, and in a manner that preserves their integrity.

requirements.name: P1.1 — Notice of privacy practices
requirements.description: The entity provides notice to data subjects about its privacy practices to meet its privacy objectives.

requirements.name: P2.1 — Choice and consent
requirements.description: The entity communicates choices available regarding personal information and obtains consent for its collection, use, retention, disclosure, and disposal.

requirements.name: P3.1 — Collection consistent with objectives
requirements.description: The entity collects personal information consistent with its objectives related to privacy.

requirements.name: P3.2 — Explicit consent for sensitive information
requirements.description: The entity obtains explicit consent for the collection, use, and disclosure of sensitive personal information.

requirements.name: P4.1 — Use limited to identified purposes
requirements.description: The entity limits the use of personal information to the purposes identified in its privacy notice and for which consent was obtained.

requirements.name: P4.2 — Retention of personal information
requirements.description: The entity retains personal information consistent with its objectives related to privacy.

requirements.name: P4.3 — Disposal of personal information
requirements.description: The entity securely disposes of personal information to meet its privacy objectives.

requirements.name: P5.1 — Access to personal information by individuals
requirements.description: The entity grants individuals access to their personal information for review and update.

requirements.name: P5.2 — Correction or amendment of personal information
requirements.description: The entity corrects, amends, or appends personal information based on information provided by individuals.

requirements.name: P6.1 — Disclosure consistent with consent
requirements.description: The entity discloses personal information to third parties only for purposes consistent with the consent obtained from individuals.

requirements.name: P6.2 — Record of authorized disclosures
requirements.description: The entity creates and retains a complete, accurate, and timely record of authorized disclosures of personal information.

requirements.name: P6.3 — Record of unauthorized disclosures
requirements.description: The entity creates and retains a complete, accurate, and timely record of detected or reported unauthorized disclosures of personal information.

requirements.name: P6.4 — Third-party compliance with privacy commitments
requirements.description: The entity obtains commitments from third parties to whom it discloses personal information that they will comply with its privacy requirements.

requirements.name: P6.5 — Notification of unauthorized disclosures by third parties
requirements.description: The entity obtains commitments from third parties to notify it of any unauthorized disclosure or use of the personal information they receive.

requirements.name: P6.6 — Breach notification
requirements.description: The entity provides notification of breaches and incidents involving personal information to affected individuals and authorities as required.

requirements.name: P6.7 — Accounting of disclosures to individuals
requirements.description: The entity provides individuals with an accounting of the personal information held and disclosures made about them upon request.

requirements.name: P7.1 — Data quality and accuracy of personal information
requirements.description: The entity maintains accurate, complete, and relevant personal information for the purposes for which it is used.

requirements.name: P8.1 — Privacy complaint management and enforcement
requirements.description: The entity implements a process for receiving, addressing, resolving, and enforcing privacy-related complaints and disputes.
